title: Combo frequency counts without target encoding
summary: Add frequency counts for selected strategy combo categories instead of extending target encoding.
rationale: Expanded combo target encoding was strongly negative, but count encoding and floor categories are useful. Combo frequency counts may capture strategy structure without target-mean leakage or sparse target overfit.
implementation_hint: Start from the parent two-seed RealMLP average code. Add one or two combo count features such as count(Race, Compound, TyreLife_cat_) or count(Race, Compound, PitStop_cat_) using train/test covariates only and no target. Keep the existing target encoding list unchanged. Do not add target-mean encodings, new model families, stacking, or calibration.
expected_effect: Improve OOF AUC if combo occurrence frequencies capture useful race-strategy context more safely than combo target means.
risk: Sparse combo counts may be unstable or redundant with existing count features.
